provider.fetchUsing: Fetch uses remote "{{remote}}" on this host.
provider.useForFetch: {{title}}: use this host for fetch or open Cloud Git
pipelines.title: Pipelines
pipelines.none: No recent runs for this repository.
pr.noBaseBranches: No other branches found for this remote. Run Fetch, or create a local base branch, then open this wizard again.
pr.baseNote: Base is the branch on the remote you want to merge into (locals and {{remote}}/… tracking names are listed).
pr.gitlabNote: GitLab only accepts a source branch that already exists on {{remote}}. If you only pushed to another remote (for example GitHub), push this branch to GitLab first, then create the merge request.
repo.label: Repository
config.pageDesc: Manage your Git identity, SSH keys, security settings, and repository behavior globally.
config.suggestions.title: Smart Suggestions
config.quickActions.title: Quick Actions
config.doctor.title: Git Doctor
config.doctor.scanDesc: Scan your config for misconfigurations, security issues, and performance gaps.
config.identity.validationPassed: Validation passed. You can apply to write global Git config.
config.security.title: Security Overview
config.security.desc: Review your Git security posture. Green = secure, Yellow = attention needed, Red = risk.
config.security.noHelper: No credential helper configured — Git will prompt for password every time.
config.security.gpgDisabled: Commits are not cryptographically signed. Others cannot verify authorship.
config.security.sslDisabled: SSL verification is disabled — vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks.
config.security.cookieSet: http.cookiefile is set — make sure this file has restricted permissions (chmod 600).
config.security.sensitiveExposed: Some sensitive keys (tokens, keys) are stored in global config. Review Config Inspector.
config.security.sensitiveClean: No obviously sensitive values detected in global config.
config.presets.beginnerSafeDesc: Sensible defaults. Safe credential storage, main branch, nano editor.
config.presets.devProDesc: Rebase workflow, auto prune, performance cache enabled.
config.presets.ossDesc: Clean merge history, compatible line endings, prune on fetch.
config.presets.highSecurityDesc: GPG signing required, SSL verification enforced.
config.presets.corporateDesc: Merge commits, signed commits, strict SSL, prune enabled.
config.behavior.presetsDesc: Apply a curated set of settings in one click. You can fine-tune individual toggles below afterwards.
config.behavior.togglesDesc: Toggle individual Git behavior settings for your local environment.
config.behavior.lineEndingDesc: core.autocrlf — normalizes line endings on checkout/commit. On Linux, Input is usually best.
config.behavior.rebaseDesc: pull.rebase=true — rewrites local commits on top of remote instead of creating a merge commit.
config.behavior.pruneBranchesDesc: fetch.prune=true — deletes local remote-tracking branches that no longer exist on the remote.
config.behavior.pruneTagsDesc: fetch.prunetags=true — also removes stale remote tags during fetch.
config.behavior.preloadDesc: core.preloadindex=true — parallelizes stat calls during git status on large repos.
config.behavior.fsCacheDesc: core.fscache=true — caches filesystem data for improved performance.
config.behavior.autoStashDesc: rebase.autostash=true — stash working directory changes before rebase, pop them after.
config.behavior.gpgDesc: commit.gpgsign=true — all commits will be cryptographically signed with your GPG key.
config.inspector.showSensitive: Show sensitive values (tokens, helpers, signing keys)
config.inspector.noMatch: No entries match your search criteria.
config.diagnostics.title: Git Doctor Diagnostics
config.diagnostics.noIssues: No issues detected. Your Git environment is healthy!
config.backups.exportDesc: Generate a secure, portable JSON snapshot of your current global Git configurations to keep as backup or sync across machines.
config.backups.importDesc: Paste a previously exported JSON array to restore your settings.
config.backups.exportSuccess: Configuration exported to clipboard as JSON.
config.backups.clipboardUnavailable: Clipboard unavailable. JSON pasted in the box below.
config.score.pristine: Your Git environment is in pristine condition.
config.score.optimization: Optimization is recommended for better security and workflow.
config.score.critical: Critical misconfigurations detected. High priority action required.
toolbar.switchToPro: Show fetch, integrate, CI checks, and PR/MR tools
toolbar.switchToSimple: Return to the simpler default layout
toolbar.simpleModeHint: Toggle Pro mode in the page header for fetch, integrate, CI, and PR/MR tools.
flow.lead.resolutionStudio: Do this now: click <chip>Open Resolution Studio</chip> in the yellow banner just below, pick a side per conflict, save, then use <chip5>+</chip5> on each file you fixed.
flow.lead.continueMerge: Do this now: click <chip>Continue</chip> (green, in the yellow banner or next to Integrate) to finish this merge/rebase step.
flow.lead.pull: Do this now: click <chip>Pull</chip> in the toolbar (same row as Fetch / Push) so your branch includes the remote commits you are behind on — then commit or push again.
flow.lead.pullSimple: The remote has new commits. Commit your work first, then use <chip>Pull</chip> in the toolbar.
flow.lead.commitMessage: Do this now: type a short summary in the <chip>Commit message</chip> field at the bottom (green outline), then <chip5>Commit</chip5>. After a commit that leaves the tree clean, the message is cleared for the next one.
flow.lead.commitMessageSimple: Type a short summary in the <chip>Commit message</chip> field at the bottom, then <chip5>Commit</chip5>.
flow.lead.commit: Do this now: click the green-outlined <chip>Commit</chip> button at the bottom — staged files (and any other local changes) go into one commit.
flow.lead.commitSimple: Review your changes, then click <chip>Commit</chip> at the bottom.
flow.lead.push: Do this now: click <chip>Push</chip> in the toolbar to publish your commits. If the host blocks the branch, follow the yellow notice for a new branch + PR/MR.
flow.lead.pushSimple: Your commits are ready. Click <chip>Push</chip> in the toolbar to publish them.
flow.body.line2: <bold>Bottom:</bold> message + <bold2>Commit</bold2>. Empty index + local changes → Commit stages all (except conflicts) then commits. Partial staging + other edits → one Commit includes the rest too.
flow.body.line3: <bold>Toolbar:</bold> <bold2>Fetch</bold2> updates remote knowledge; <chip4>Pull</chip4> when you are behind; <mono6>Push</mono6> when you are ready to publish.
flow.body.line4Behind: Behind by <chip>{{behind}}</chip> — use <chip3>Pull</chip3> first. Use <chip5>Integrate / Sync</chip5> only if you intentionally want to merge/rebase another ref instead of a straight pull.
flow.body.line4Ahead: Ahead by <chip>{{ahead}}</chip> — after commits are clean, <chip3>Push</chip3> sends them upstream; <chip5>New PR</chip5> opens a review on the host.
flow.body.line4Even: Even with your upstream — you do <chip>not</chip> need <chip3>Integrate / Sync</chip3> unless you are merging another branch/ref (e.g. <mono6>origin/main</mono6>) into this one. To publish new commits, use <chip8>Push</chip8>; to start review, use <chip10>New PR</chip10>.
flow.body.line5: <bold>Integrate / Sync</bold> opens a merge/rebase wizard: you enter the <bold2>other</bold2> branch or remote ref (e.g. <chip4>main</chip4>, <mono6>origin/main</mono6>) to bring into your current branch. It is <chip8>not</chip8> the same as Push or opening a PR.
flow.body.line6: Stash icon: pop the latest stash when you need it.
flow.body.conflict1: Conflicted files show status <chip>C</chip> (often red). Click one to read the diff.
flow.body.conflict2: After Resolution Studio (or your editor), stage with <chip>+</chip> so Git sees the fix.
flow.body.conflict3: If <chip>Continue</chip> is missing, refresh with <chip3>Fetch</chip3> or select another file until the banner updates.
flow.body.operation1: When the index is clean for this step, Continue lets Git move forward or finish.
flow.body.operation2: <bold>Abort</bold> cancels the whole {{op}} (you may need to tidy the tree afterward).
flow.extra.stageInfo: You can stage with <chip>+</chip> first, or go straight to a commit message + <chip3>Commit</chip3> — both work.
page.title: Git VCS
page.subtitle: Status, diffs, stage, commit, fetch, pull, push, merge, rebase, and stash pop. HTTPS remotes use tokens from <link>Connected accounts</link>.
page.subtitleSimple: Edit files, commit when ready, then pull or push to sync. Click Enable Pro mode (top right) for fetch, integrate, CI, and PR/MR tools.
ciChecks.fetchingStatus: Fetching merge status and latest pipeline jobs…
ciChecks.noJobsSubline: GitLab did not return job rows for this branch (pipelines may be disabled or the token cannot read jobs).
ciChecks.mergeTitle.hasConflicts: Resolve merge conflicts before merging on the server.
ciChecks.mergeTitle.hasFailed: Fix failing checks before merging on the server.
ciChecks.mergeTitle.ready: Merge this pull request on GitHub (requires permission).
ciChecks.conflictBanner.title: This branch has conflicts that must be resolved
conflictResolver.noDataFound: No conflict data found for this file.
conflictResolver.wholeFileDesc: This file has a conflict but no markers were found (likely a binary file or structural conflict). Pick which version to keep.
conflictResolver.useEditedTitle: Use the manually edited merged content
conflictResolver.clickToAdd: Click to add to merged result
conflictWizard.overview.title: Resolve Merge Conflicts
conflictWizard.summary.title: All Conflicts Resolved
conflictWizard.summary.subtitle: Ready to continue with merge
conflictWizard.summary.nextStep: Next step: Stage and commit your changes
conflictWizard.summary.allResolved: All conflicts have been resolved. You can now continue with the merge or rebase.
diffPanel.selectFile: Select a file to view its diff
diffPanel.binaryFile: Binary file changes cannot be displayed here.
diffPanel.noDiff: No textual diff (empty or new empty file).
dirtyCheckout.bodyReason: because local changes would be overwritten. Choose how to proceed.
dirtyCheckout.cannotParseFiles: (Could not parse file list from Git output — stash still includes tracked changes and, if enabled, untracked files.)
dirtyCheckout.helpText.before: After switching, restore your shelved work with
integrateWizard.title: Integrate Changes
integrateWizard.subtitle.before: Bringing updates from another branch into
integrateWizard.targetLabel: Branch or remote ref to merge/rebase from
integrateWizard.targetHint.before: Choose a branch below or type any ref. That ref is merged/rebased into
integrateWizard.targetHint.after: . For Push or New PR only, close this dialog.
integrateWizard.remoteSuggestions: Remote suggestions are {{host}} remotes only (same host as your fetch remote).
integrateWizard.pickBranchAriaLabel: Pick a branch to integrate from
integrateWizard.refPlaceholderMenu: Matches the menu above; edit here if needed
integrateWizard.refAriaLabel: Branch or remote ref to merge or rebase from
integrateWizard.merge.label: Merge
integrateWizard.merge.desc: Combine histories. Creates a merge commit.
integrateWizard.rebase.label: Rebase
integrateWizard.rebase.desc: Replay your work on top of the target history.
integrateWizard.tip.label: Tip:
integrateWizard.tip.text: LuminaDev will automatically open the Resolution Studio if conflicts occur during the process.
integrateWizard.scope.label: Scope:
integrateWizard.scope.after: in this clone (local merge/rebase). It does not merge a pull or merge request on GitHub/GitLab—you still push, then complete the MR/PR on the website (or merge via API) when you are ready.
stateBanner.mergeConflicts: You have {{count}} file(s) with conflicts. Use the Resolution Studio to pick which changes to keep.
stateBanner.mergeAllResolved: All conflicts are resolved! You can now conclude the merge to save your changes.
stateBanner.rebaseConflicts: Rebase paused due to {{count}} conflicted file(s).
stateBanner.rebaseNoConflicts: No conflicts remaining. Continue rebase to apply the next commit.
stateBanner.unmergedBody: Git still lists {{count}} file(s) with merge conflicts.
assistant.page.title: Git Assistant
assistant.page.subtitle: Set up once, save your work locally, and share online when you are ready — same calm flow as the rest of Lumina.
assistant.rail.hint: Status indicators — not a required order. Pull before commit anytime via git pull.
assistant.next.allGood: All good — nothing else to do here.
assistant.next.connect_cloud.hint: You have local commits ready to send. Connect GitHub or GitLab in Settings when you want to push (optional for local-only work).
assistant.next.open_project.hint: Choose a project folder to start tracking changes.
assistant.next.open_editor.hint: Two people edited the same file. Resolve in your IDE, then return here — status refreshes automatically.
assistant.next.continue_merge.hint: Finish merging in your editor, then continue here.
assistant.next.pull.hint: Someone else updated the project first. Pull before you commit or push.
assistant.next.commit.hint: Commit the staged changes you selected.
assistant.next.commit_message.hint: Write a commit message before git commit.
assistant.next.push.hint: Send your commits to the remote.
assistant.setup.title: Setup checklist
assistant.setup.subtitle: One-time host Git configuration. A cloud account is optional for local commits.
assistant.setup.identity: Your name and email are set
assistant.setup.cloud: Cloud account connected (optional for local work)
assistant.project.title: Your project
assistant.project.subtitle: Open a folder or clone a repository to track changes.
assistant.clone.parentHint: Pick the parent folder where the repo should live (e.g. ~/Projects), not the repo folder itself. Lumina creates a subfolder from the URL name.
assistant.clone.openedExisting: That repository is already on disk — opened the existing folder.
assistant.project.empty: Open or clone a project to see changes here.
assistant.changes.title: Changed files ({{count}})
assistant.changes.clean: No local changes — working tree clean.
assistant.changes.previewEmpty: No diff to show for this file.
assistant.changes.previewBinary: Binary file — open in your editor to inspect.
assistant.branch.sectionSubtitle: Switch or create a branch without leaving this page.
assistant.save.title: Save & share
assistant.save.subtitle: Commit locally anytime. Pull or push when your remote is in play.
assistant.share.title: Share online
assistant.share.subtitle: Optional — connect GitHub or GitLab when you want to push.
assistant.merge.title: Merge in progress
assistant.merge.subtitle: Finish in your editor, then continue or abort here.
assistant.cloud.prompt: A cloud account is optional for local commits. Connect GitHub or GitLab when you want to push.
assistant.editorRefresh: Refreshed after you returned from your editor.
assistant.mergeInProgress: A merge or rebase is in progress. Finish in your editor, then continue or abort.
assistant.setup.doctorError: Could not run Git Doctor. Try Fix now or Settings.
assistant.setup.doctorNoIssue: No extra issues for this item.
assistant.share.connectedSubtitle: Your work is on {{host}} — open the repo or start a merge request in the browser.
assistant.share.pushedHint: Latest commits are on the remote. Open {{host}} to create a pull request or review changes.
assistant.share.noRemote: Connect a GitHub or GitLab remote (origin) to open the repo in the browser.
assistant.remoteUpdates.title: Updates on the remote
assistant.remoteUpdates.body: The server has {{count}} new commit(s) you do not have yet. Get latest before you edit files to avoid merge conflicts.
assistant.behind.title: Get latest before you send
assistant.behind.body: The remote has {{count}} commit(s) you do not have yet. Pull first, then send your work.
assistant.push.success: Sent to the remote. Open on {{host}} to review or open a pull request.
assistant.pr.noBranch: Open a project to create a pull request.
assistant.pr.onDefaultBranch: You are on {{branch}}. Use the Branch section to switch to a feature branch, then create a pull request into {{base}}.
assistant.pr.connectCloud: Connect GitHub or GitLab in Settings to create the pull request from here.
assistant.pr.title: Pull request
assistant.pr.subtitle: Merge {{head}} into {{base}} on {{host}}.
assistant.pr.pushFirst: Push your commits first, then create the pull request.
assistant.pr.noUpstream: This branch is not on origin yet. Push with upstream (e.g. git push -u origin <branch>), then create or compare the pull request.
assistant.pr.behindBase: {{base}} has {{count}} commit(s) you do not have yet. Pull first so the PR stays mergeable.
assistant.pr.bodyPlaceholder: PR template (Summary, Scope Check, Test Evidence, Docs Check, Notes)
assistant.pr.titleRequired: Add a title for the pull request.
assistant.pr.createFailed: Could not create the pull request.
assistant.pr.openLinkManually: Use “Open link again” below if nothing appeared.
assistant.pr.wrongProvider: Remote is {{host}} but the connected account is {{connected}}. Connect {{host}} under Settings → Connected accounts.
